title: NASA interested in Hubble reboost if costs can be reduced
summary: NASA is considering a reboost mission for the Hubble Space Telescope if the costs can be reduced. The agency is already preparing a similar attempt for another spacecraft in a decaying orbit.

title: NASA concerns about Russian repairs prompted ISS safe haven decision
summary: NASA directed ISS astronauts to take shelter in a Crew Dragon spacecraft due to concerns over Russian cosmonauts' repairs, which the agency deemed posed an 'elevated risk' to the station.

title: Leaf Space partners with D-Orbit and EnduroSat to test connectivity service
summary: Leaf Space has partnered with D-Orbit and EnduroSat to test a new space connectivity service called TreeNet, aimed at improving space communications. The service was unveiled on May 27.

title: Astronauts briefly shelter in Dragon during ISS leak repair
summary: Astronauts on the International Space Station sheltered in a Dragon spacecraft while cosmonauts repaired an air leak in a Russian module. The incident occurred on June 5.

title: Next Ariane 6 launch to carry 36 Amazon Leo satellites using upgraded boosters
summary: The next Ariane 6 launch will carry 36 Amazon Leo satellites, marking the largest number of broadband satellites flown by Amazon to date. This mission will also be the first Ariane 6 launch to utilize upgraded boosters.

title: Let’s build the moon base, but not lose sight of Mars
summary: NASA and the space industry are experiencing significant progress, highlighted by the Artemis 2 crew's lunar flyby. The article emphasizes the importance of building a moon base while maintaining focus on Mars exploration.

title: Qianfan constellation deployment hits 200 satellites with Long March 8 and 6A launches
summary: The Qianfan constellation, led by Shanghai, has reached 200 satellites following two recent Long March launches (8 and 6A). This marks a significant milestone in the rapid deployment of the constellation.

title: Satellite maker Apex’s valuation rises to $2.3 billion after latest $200 million raise
summary: Apex, a satellite manufacturer, raised $200 million in a funding round that nearly doubled its valuation to $2.3 billion. The company plans to expand its in-house satellite production capabilities with the new funds.

title: The Exploration Company completes drop test of Nyx capsule
summary: The Exploration Company successfully conducted a drop test of its Nyx spacecraft, designed for cargo transport to low Earth orbit. The test is a precursor to a planned 2028 test flight.

title: Axiom Space adds more than $175 million to funding round
summary: Axiom Space raised over $175 million in its latest funding round, with Japan’s largest bank joining as an investor. The company announced this funding boost on June 4.

title: Who is Lobo, the cigar-smoking alien bounty hunter in 'Supergirl'?
summary: Jason Momoa will star as Lobo, a cigar-smoking alien bounty hunter, in the upcoming 'Supergirl' series. Lobo is a well-known character from DC Comics, often depicted as a violent and chaotic antihero.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 213 — Live From ISDC With Gerry Griffin
summary: The latest episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a live discussion with Apollo legend Gerry Griffin at the International Space Development Conference (ISDC) in McLean, Virginia. The episode is hosted by Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik.

title: Neil deGrasse Tyson narrates new trailer for upcoming Broadway musical, 'Galileo'
summary: Neil deGrasse Tyson narrates a new trailer for the Broadway musical 'Galileo', featuring Raúl Esparza in the lead role as the Renaissance astronomer. The show is set to premiere this fall.

title: I resisted smart telescopes for years — then one changed my life
summary: The article discusses the author's positive experience with smart telescopes, highlighting their benefits for deep-sky stargazing during spring. It emphasizes the appeal of modern astronomy technology for enthusiasts.

title: Going supersonic! NASA's X-59 jet breaks sound barrier for the 1st time
summary: NASA's X-59 jet successfully broke the sound barrier for the first time on June 5, achieving a top speed of approximately Mach 1.1. This milestone marks a significant step in supersonic flight technology.

title: DC Studios lights the fuse on a sensational final 'Supergirl' trailer and it's totally peak (video)
summary: DC Studios released the final trailer for the upcoming 'Supergirl' project, teasing a dramatic conclusion. The trailer highlights differing perspectives on heroism between the main characters.

title: Inside the cruel confines of Apple TV's 'Star City' with star Rhys Ifans and series creators (interview)
summary: Apple TV's 'Star City' explores danger on the ground, contrasting with the space peril in 'For All Mankind'. The series features Rhys Ifans and its creators in an interview.

title: Scientists find wind blowing from our Milky Way's black hole after half-century search: 'There it is'
summary: Astronomers have discovered powerful winds emanating from Sagittarius A*, the supermassive black hole at the center of the Milky Way, after a 50-year search. This finding provides new insights into the behavior and influence of black holes on their surroundings.

title: 'Masters of the Universe' is spacier than we expected (and it's awesome)
summary: A recent article highlights the unexpected space-related elements in the movie 'Masters of the Universe' and lists its 10 most notable space moments. The film is praised for its impressive space-themed scenes.

title: After New Glenn anomaly, Blue Origin keeps focus on upcoming Blue Moon and Mars missions
summary: Blue Origin remains committed to its Blue Moon lunar lander and Mars missions despite the recent failure of the New Glenn rocket during hotfire testing at Launch Complex 36. The company is focusing on recovery and future objectives.

title: The Exploration Company advances Nyx parachute recovery
summary: The Exploration Company (TEC) has made progress in advancing the parachute recovery system for its reusable Nyx spacecraft. This development marks a key milestone in the spacecraft's design and testing phase.

title: Space Force evaluating anomaly-related damage, future danger areas
summary: Space Launch Delta 45 (SLD 45) is evaluating damage and potential future risks related to an anomaly. The Eastern Range, operated by SLD 45, has been cleared for operations.

title: Artemis III’s remaining solid rocket booster segments shipped from Utah to Florida
summary: The remaining solid rocket booster segments for NASA's Artemis III mission have been transported from Utah to Florida. This marks a key step in preparations for the upcoming lunar mission despite ongoing uncertainties about timelines and landers.

title: Blue Origin initiates LC-36 recovery, targets RTF by end of 2026
summary: Blue Origin has begun inspection, repair, and reconstruction activities at Launch Complex-36, aiming for a return-to-flight (RTF) by the end of 2026.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese rockets to launch internet satellites to LEO
summary: SpaceX and Chinese rockets are set to launch internet satellites to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) this week, with six missions planned from five different launch providers. The launches highlight the growing competition in the global satellite internet sector.

title: Ancient black hole sheds new light on Webb’s Little Red Dots
summary: An international team used the James Webb Space Telescope to study an ancient black hole, providing new insights into 'Webb’s Little Red Dots'. This discovery enhances our understanding of early cosmic structures.

title: Blue Origin’s New Glenn explodes during Static Fire test at LC-36
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded during a static fire test at Launch Complex 36. The anomaly marks a significant setback for the company's orbital launch ambitions.

title: Rovers and Drones laying the foundations for NASA Moon Base
summary: NASA has outlined initial missions for its Moon Base initiative, utilizing rovers and drones to lay the groundwork. The initiative involves rebranding existing Commercial Lunar missions.

title: Following Starship V3 debut, SpaceX prepares for follow up
summary: SpaceX conducted the first flight of the Starship Version 3 this week. The company is now preparing for a follow-up mission.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers who contribute innovative ideas.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space-related activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses a sturdy and sensitive space industry component. The image likely depicts this component.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving a parachute system, referred to as 'the great parachute bake-out,' likely related to a space mission or landing technology. The focus appears to be on thermal or environmental testing of the parachute.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA's ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission is preparing a 35-meter parachute for Mars by sterilizing it to prevent microbial contamination. The parachute, the largest ever for Mars, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: AstroForge Completes its DeepSpace-2 Spacecraft
summary: AstroForge has completed its DeepSpace-2 spacecraft, which aims to demonstrate the company's capability to rendezvous with an asteroid. This mission sets the foundation for future asteroid mining efforts planned before the end of the decade.

title: Apex Raises $200M At $2.3B Valuation
summary: Apex has raised an additional $200 million, bringing its valuation to $2.3 billion. This marks the company's third $200 million funding round in approximately 14 months.

title: US Commercial Space Stations Expand to Europe
summary: The U.S. commercial space stations are expanding their reach into Europe, ensuring continued European access to Low Earth Orbit (LEO) as the International Space Station (ISS) nears the end of its operational life.

title: Recapping Planet’s Q1 With CFO and President Ashley Johnson
summary: Planet's Q1 recap highlights its differentiation in revenue recognition compared to hardware-focused companies. The discussion emphasizes Planet's approach to handling outcomes in its business model.

title: The Exploration Company Completes Nyx Drop Test
summary: The Exploration Company successfully completed a drop test for its Nyx capsule on May 19, using a dedicated vehicle to assess parachute performance and landing dynamics. The test aimed to validate controlled Earth re-entry and landing capabilities.

title: Austria’s First Commercial Sat is Ready for Launch
summary: Austria's first commercial satellite, Oasis Alpha, has been shipped by Vienna-based startup Tumbleweed to launch integrator Exolaunch for a planned July launch.

title: Muon Space Unveils New, Larger Satellite Bus
summary: Muon Space has introduced a new, larger satellite bus named Condor-Ultra, which is three times the size of its predecessor Condor-XL. This satellite bus is designed to cater to the emerging orbital data center market.

title: NASA Reverts to Original CLD Procurement Plan
summary: NASA has decided to revert to its original Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLD) procurement plan, aligning with industry feedback. This move aims to shape the future of lunar payload services through a commercial strategy.

title: Blue Origin Commits to Return to Flight This Year
summary: Blue Origin has announced plans to resume flight operations by the end of the year after assessing launch pad damage. CEO Dave Limp confirmed the return-to-flight commitment on social media.

title: NDAA Would Cut SDA, RCO Offices
summary: The House's annual defense authorization bill proposes eliminating two Pentagon offices, the Space Development Agency (SDA) and the Rapid Capabilities Office (RCO), which are focused on accelerating space acquisition. This move could impact the Pentagon's efforts to modernize and streamline space programs.

title: FCC Gives Amazon Leo 50% Deployment Waiver, With Conditions on Spectrum Priority
summary: The FCC granted Amazon a 50% deployment waiver for its Project Kuiper constellation by July, but imposed conditions on spectrum priority. The decision allows Amazon to accelerate its satellite launch timeline with regulatory oversight.

title: Apex Hits $2.3B Valuation With Latest Funding Raise
summary: Satellite bus manufacturer Apex Space raised an additional $200 million in capital at a $2.3 billion valuation. This funding round more than doubles the company's previous valuation.

title: Aalyria to Support Orchestration for NASA SCaN Program
summary: NASA selected Aalyria to support orchestration for its Space Communications and Navigation (SCaN) Program, beginning with the PExT demonstration mission. The Polylingual Experimental Terminal (PExT) mission has been launched as part of this initiative.

title: Tototheo Global Taps Eutelsat for LEO Service for Maritime
summary: Tototheo Global has partnered with Eutelsat to provide Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite services for maritime applications. This collaboration aims to enhance connectivity and communication solutions for the maritime industry.

title: NGA Awards Planet $22M Luno B Option for Maritime Domain Awareness
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) awarded Planet Labs a $22 million option for AI-enabled maritime domain awareness data analytics. This extends a contract started in the previous year to support U.S. Combatant Commands.

title: Infinite Orbits and Open Cosmos Plan ‘Tom & Jerry’ Space Rendezvous Mission
summary: Infinite Orbits has selected Open Cosmos to develop two satellites, 'Tom & Jerry', for a maneuvering and inspection mission in Low-Earth Orbit (LEO). The mission aims to demonstrate space rendezvous capabilities.

title: Mbryonics and NTT to Collaborate on Optical Communications Infrastructure
summary: Mbryonics, an Irish photonics company, will collaborate with NTT to develop a high-speed optical communications backbone for space. The partnership is formalized under a new Memorandum of Understanding (MoU).

title: Muon Space Debuts High-Power ‘Ultra’ Satellite Designed for Orbital Data Centers
summary: Muon Space introduced a new high-power satellite platform named 'Ultra' aimed at supporting orbital data centers. The first mission for this platform is scheduled for 2025.

title: Beyond Gravity Releases New Propulsion Pointing Mechanism
summary: Beyond Gravity has introduced a new ultra-light propulsion pointing mechanism designed for high maneuverability in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) constellations. The two-axis mechanism aims to enhance satellite positioning and performance.

title: Applied Aerospace & Defense IPOs to Raise $650M
summary: Applied Aerospace & Defense successfully launched an IPO on the New York Stock Exchange, raising $650 million by offering 32.5 million shares. The company, operating in the aerospace and defense sector, began trading on Wednesday.
